node.description: Register to control the start of runs and run mode
Bit0 = Ethernet override 1=running, 0=not running
Bit2..1 = Run signal select 3=VHDCI run allowing for short pause 2=VXI synced run, 1=Ethernet run signal 0=VHDCI run signal
Bit3 = Increment frame counter when run signal low 0=increment counter, 1=don't increment counter
Frame sync source select 0=Internal, 1=SMP, 2=TOF
Internal frame sync period in µS (0x04E20=20ms,0x18600=100ms)
Frame Sync time delay from frame source trigger (µS)
Fast chopper 0 window delay, 20 ns steps
Fast chopper 0 window length, 20 ns steps
Fast chopper 1 window delay, 20 ns steps
Fast chopper 1 window length, 20 ns steps
Fast chopper 2 window delay, 20 ns steps
Fast chopper 2 window length, 20 ns steps
Fast chopper 3 window delay, 20 ns steps
Fast chopper 3 window length, 20 ns steps
Period control register
Bit1 = End run at end of period sequence
bit5..4 = Period mode 00: computer, 01: look up table 10: not used), 11: external
Bit6 = End run after last period sequence
Current Period set by computer
Period number limit
Period sequence limit
Amount of time to wait when VXI_RUN goes to low before stopping the run (to account for pauses)
Enable firmware that corrects the frame time due to inaccuracy of board oscillators
Register to set the frame length for the time correction process, time in ns. x3B9ACA00=1second (PPS), x01312D00=20ms (TS1), x05F5E100-100ms (TS2)
Register control if status packet uses CRC check
Controls if the RX path should perform status packet check
Controls if the TX path should perform status packet check
A counter showing the amount of CRC errors detected
A single bit flag that shows if a CRC is present
A single bit flag showing if the CRC is correct
Current status packet time - years
Current status packet time - days
Current status packet time - hours
Current status packet time - minutes
Current status packet time - seconds
Current status packet time - milliseconds
Current status packet time - microseconds
Current status packet time - nanoseconds
Board and Serial Number
Board Version
Board number
Register to load the board serial number into
Firmware number version
3 digit hex readable micro
3 digit hex readable minor
2 digit hex readable major
Build date in hex readable form [year]-[month]-[day]
2 digit hex readable day
2 digit hex readable month
4 digit hex readable year
Build time in hex readable form [hour]:[minute]:[seconds]
2 digit hex readable seconds
2 digit hex readable minute
2 digit hex readable hour
Abbreviated Git hash for commit where bit-stream was compiled (padded to 32bit)
UDP core soft resets
UDP core AXI lite active low reset
RX slave reset for the UDP core
TX master reset for the UDP core
eth slave reset for the UDP core
eth master reset for the UDP core
ipv4 slave reset for the UDP core
ipv4 master reset for the UDP core
UDP slave reset for the UDP core
UDP master reset for the UDP core
Ethernet phy reset
Component soft resets

axi4 interconnect reset

axi4 firmware number reset
axi4 to XADC
fpga monitor reset
Bit[0] = ADC convert start register(3), Bit[1] = Enable temperature update logic, Bit[17:2] = Wait cycle for temperature update
The 12-bit MSB justified result of on-device temperature measurement is stored in this register (Temp_reading(15 downto 4)*503.975/4096)-273.15
The 12-bit MSB justified result of on-device VCCINT supply monitor measurement is stored in this register Volt_reading(15 downto 4)/4096)*3
The 12-bit MSB justified result of on-device VCCAUX supply monitor measurement is stored in this register
The 12-bit MSB justified maximum temperature measurement.
The 12-bit MSB justified maximum VCCINT measurement.
The 12-bit MSB justified maximum VCCAUX measurement.
The 12-bit MSB justified maximum VBRAM measurement.
The 12-bit MSB justified minimum temperature measurement.
The 12-bit MSB justified minimum VCCINT measurement.
The 12-bit MSB justified minimum VCCAUX measurement.
The 12-bit MSB justified minimum VBRAM measurement.
The 12-bit MSB justified alarm upper temperature threshold register.
The 12-bit MSB justified alarm upper VCCINT threshold register.
The 12-bit MSB justified alarm upper VCCAUX threshold register.
The 12-bit MSB justified alarm lower temperature threshold register.
The 12-bit MSB justified alarm lower VCCINT threshold register.
The 12-bit MSB justified alarm lower VCCAUX threshold register.

Enables Broadcast Recieving
Enables ARPs

Check Source IP address Field Of Incoming Packet
Check Dest UDP Port Field Of Incoming Packet
Check Source UDP Port Field Of Incoming Packet

Remove The Header From Incoming Unsupported IPv4 Data
Remove The Header From Incoming Unsupported Ethernet Data
Check Received Packets Total IP Length and Cut off Extra Padding
Used To Add Extra Delay Between Packets In Tx Path
UDP Core Control Register
Use Fixed Packet Size In Tx For Outgoing Packets - Currently Unused
Set UDP Checksum to Zero - Currently Unused
LUT Mode Enable - Use To Set Dst Addresses Of Outgoing UDP Packets From LUT
Use Bits 15:0 of tuser for destination UDP port of outgoing packets
Use Bits 31:16 of tuser for source UDP port of outgoing packets
